# Questions in Livestream 130

Live Stream #130: Million Dollar Questions

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=652" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:52</a> | Did the Great Queen take precedence over the firstborn of a secondary wife in Egyptian succession? | Yes; the son of the Great Queen normally has the stronger claim to inherit the throne. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:25</a> | Have you seen the 2016 anime Erased? | Yes; he calls it a heavy anime with dark themes including child abuse and murder. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=964" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:04</a> | How did the Israelites worship while enslaved in Egypt? | He says they worshiped pagan Canaanite deities, including figures such as Rephaim and Astarte. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=1517" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:17</a> | Are there any apologetics programs you actually recommend? | He recommends Southern Evangelical Seminary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=1902" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:42</a> | Does Romans 5:12 preclude pre-fall human death? | He reads the passage as focused on death through sin in Adam, not as a simple pre-fall animal or human death prooftext. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=2225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:05</a> | How would biblical authors have defined the term human? | He says the question is difficult because biblical categories are not modern biological taxonomy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=2577" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:57</a> | Which is more fun to visit, Egypt or Israel? | He is biased toward Egypt because he has been there many times and has not been to Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=2860" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:40</a> | Do Jeremiah 7:22 and Amos 5:25 saying Israel did not sacrifice in the wilderness prove the prophets lacked the Pentateuch? | No; he says that reading misses the point, with Amos 5:25 clearer than Jeremiah 7:22. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=3411" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:51</a> | What does Nahum 2:2 mean by God restoring the majesty of Jacob as well as Israel? | He treats Jacob and Israel as functioning in parallel in the poetic couplet. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=3652" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:52</a> | What are your biggest concerns with Reformed theology, specifically the Five Points of Dort? | He says he is not deeply familiar with Dort and gives a cautious answer rather than a full critique. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=4185" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:45</a> | Does Reformed theology have a balanced view of God's sovereignty and human will, especially libertarian versus compatibilist will? | He discusses branches of Calvinism and says the balance depends on how those categories are handled. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=4535" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:35</a> | Does Daniel 12:3 connect resurrection with stars because resurrected people become angelic beings? | No; he says the text uses shining or glory imagery and does not require becoming angels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=4818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:18</a> | Would you do a livestream with David Wood to explain your view of eternal punishment? | Yes, he says he would be willing to do that. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=5603" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:23</a> | In Heiser's Deuteronomy 32 worldview, did Eastern religions develop their idols and ideas about God through the gods that led them? | He treats the question as related to divine-council theology and the nations, but not as a simple one-to-one origin story. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=6562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:22</a> | What are the heavenly bodies in 1 Corinthians 15:40? | He reads the passage by comparing earthly and heavenly kinds of glory in 1 Corinthians 15. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=6901" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:01</a> | What are your thoughts on Molinism? | He says he is not very familiar with Molinism and cannot give a strong evaluation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=7401" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:21</a> | Did judges judge priests, or was it only God? | Judges sometimes acted like a supreme court, so it was not only direct divine judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=7655" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:35</a> | What does modalism deny God of, and what are your best arguments against it? | Modalism collapses the three persons into one role-switching person; he argues Scripture shows Father, Son, and Spirit as distinct persons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=7965" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:45</a> | How would you answer Oneness Pentecostals who say the term person is not in the Bible? | The term is a later vocabulary label, but Scripture portrays the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit personally. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=8500" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:40</a> | What about the argument that Jesus never existed because there was no J in Greek? | He rejects it as a bad linguistic argument; Jesus is the English form that comes through Greek and Latin from Hebrew/Aramaic forms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=8722" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:22</a> | What are your thoughts on The Chosen using poetic creative license with the Bible? | Creative license is inevitable in visual adaptation, but it becomes a problem when it reads ideas into the text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=9276" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:36</a> | Do you prefer The Hobbit or The Lord of the Rings, and is Warhammer heretical? | He prefers The Lord of the Rings and does not treat Warhammer as heretical in itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=9505" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:25</a> | What is the biblical evidence for Adam and Eve as historical individuals? | He says there is no direct external evidence, but Scripture treats them as real progenitor figures in a mytho-historical narrative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=10017" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:57</a> | Is the name Israel a combination of Isis, Ra, and El? | No; he says Israel is Semitic and the Isis/Ra/El claim fails linguistically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=10436" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:56</a> | What is the best argument atheists make against Christianity? | He says the strongest "argument" is usually not reason but desire: not wanting to live under God's righteousness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=10619" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:59</a> | Have you read Jewish arguments against Christianity and for Jesus not being the Messiah? | Yes; he says Jewish apologetics against Christianity is generally very weak. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=11322" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:42</a> | What is the psychology of apostasy? | He says apostasy often begins with failure to grow in discipleship, leaving people vulnerable to falling away. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=12225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:45</a> | Is it true that Jews once believed in the two powers based on Daniel 7:13-14? | Yes; he says two-powers theology existed in Second Temple Judaism and is found in Daniel and other texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=12557" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:17</a> | How much controversy is there in Christianity over the identity of the Angel of the Lord, and what is the significance of him going up with the offering in Judges 13:20? | He treats Judges 13 as important for identifying the Angel of the Lord and then discusses broader Christian disagreement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=12918" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:18</a> | If testimony is an epistemological standard, can resurrection-magic testimonies cast doubt on the Resurrection? | He says that would require resurrection magic to be demonstrated, not merely asserted by testimony. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/9nYsP-vzSXQ?t=14040" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:00</a> | Will you play guitar for us? | No; he says he has standards and will not play guitar on the stream. |
